meaning: 伊斯肯德仑[土耳其南部港市](即亚历山大勒塔)
examples: An ancient town of southeast Asia Minor near modern-day Iskenderun, Turkey. Alexander the Great defeated Darius III of Persia here in 333 b. c.
Turkey's state-run Anadolu news agency late on Wednesday said some 30 military vehicles, including heavy armor, had moved to the border from Iskenderun base on the Mediterranean.
American warships, loaded with combat materiel, began docking at Turkey's south-eastern port of Iskenderun.
The first battery became operational last Saturday in the city of Adana, NATO said, and more equipment arrived Wednesday in the port city of Iskenderun.
